title: planchonella baueri
intro: Planchonella baueri, also known as the Queensland walnut or black walnut, is a species of evergreen tree native to Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems, fever, and respiratory issues. The seeds of Planchonella baueri contain a compound called juglone, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The seeds can be prepared as an herbal medicine through various methods, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
